data.title: Global Markets Boosted by Fed Rate-Cut Hopes; U.S. Markets Closed for Thanksgiving
data.description:
By Dow Jones Newswires Staff

Global markets rose on increased hopes of a Federal Reserve rate cut next month, and as investor optimism over artificial intelligence returns. U.S. markets are closed Thursday for the Thanksgiving holiday and are open for a shortened session Friday.

  • U.S. Futures for the Dow Jones Industrial Index and S&P were up 0.05% and 0.06%, respectively, while the tech-heavy Nasdaq was up 0.09% ahead of the market reopen Friday. All indexes closed higher Wednesday for the fourth session in a row, boosted by Microsoft, CoreWeave and Dell.
  • Asian indexes closed mostly higher, with Japan and South Korea seeing steady gains led by technology stocks. The Nikkei closed 1.2% higher, and the Kospi added 0.7%. However, indexes in China were mixed due to some profit-taking setting in on tech stocks. The ChiNext and Shenzhen closed 0.4% and 0.1% lower. The Shanghai composite index is 0.3% higher, while Hong Kong's Hang Seng is trading flat.
  • In Europe Germany's DAX was up 0.4%, France's CAC 40 rose 0.2% and London's premier FTSE 100 index was up 0.1%. The Stoxx 600 index was up 0.15%. Sportswear-maker Puma was leading the risers--up 13%--on a report that China's sports apparel company Anta Sports was among those exploring a potential takeover of the German company.
  • The dollar traded steady but remains near a one-week low reached overnight on increased bets that the Federal Reserve will cut interest rates further. Recent comments from Fed policymakers, including John Williams, Mary Daly and Christopher Waller, confirmed expectations for another rate cut in December. With the U.S. Treasury and stocks markets closed Thursday for Thanksgiving, liquidity will be thinner into the weekend, with the dollar potentially stabilizing after this week's falls, ING's Francesco Pesole says in a note. The DXY dollar index trades flat at 99.615, near the overnight low of 99.406.
  • Yields on U.K. government bonds were little changed in early trade as investors digest the fiscal measures outline in Wednesday's budget announcement. Gilt yields fell following the budget statement and news that the fiscal headroom had doubled to 22 billion pounds from nine billion pounds at the previous statement in March. The 10-year gilt yields edged up 0.4 basis points to 4.427%, Tradeweb data show.
  • Sterling hovered close to a four-week high against the dollar and euro as investors continue to express relief that Wednesday's U.K. budget delivered no major surprises. U.K. Treasury chief Rachel Reeves announced a series of tax rises to fix the public finances, as widely expected. Sterling traded flat at $1.3238, close to a high of $1.3268 reached earlier. The euro traded flat at 0.8750 pounds, near an earlier low of 0.8741
  • Eurozone government bond yields were marginally lower in opening trade. The 10-year Bund yield fell 0.8 basis points to 2.667%, while the 10-year French OAT yield was 0.5 basis points lower at 3.394%, according to Tradeweb.
  • The 10-year Treasury yield closed at 3.991% on Wednesday, according to Tradeweb. Trading in U.S. Treasurys is closed on Thursday due to the Thanksgiving holiday.
  • Oil prices were steady as traders take stock of developments to end the Russia-Ukraine war. Brent crude and WTI were both down 0.1% to $62.46 and $58.59 a barrel, respectively. U.S. envoy Steve Witkoff will hold talks in Russia next week, while Ukraine said good foundations have been laid toward reaching an agreement.
  • Gold prices were steady but remain elevated on strengthening bets that the U.S. Federal Reserve will cut rates in December, MUFG's Soojin Kim writes. Futures in New York were down 0.1% at $4,196.20 a troy ounce.

Write to Barcelona Editors at barcelonaeditors@dowjones.com

data.title: Margins Cracked, Not the Business: Why I'm Buying Axon Here
data.description:
If you've seen a police officer on the street lately, chances are you've seen Axon's products. Whether it's the body camera on their vest or a TASER at their side, Axon gear is embedded across U.S. law enforcement. What started as a hardware company is now a full-stack public safety platform, expanding into AI, dispatch software, drones, and cloud-based digital evidence.

The third quarter print showed mixed signals. Margins slipped, GAAP profits turned negative, and the stock closed down more than 10%. In this article, I'll break down Axon's business, dissect the latest financials, reassess the valuation, and ask whether the drop presents a long-term opportunity or a warning sign.

Business Overview and Moat

Axon develops and sells integrated public safety technology aimed at protecting life and making the bullet obsolete. The company's mission, to protect life, has driven it to expand beyond its original TASER devices into a broad ecosystem of law enforcement technology. Today, Axon's product portfolio includes smart weapons, sensors, cameras, and cloud-based software.

Source: Axon

The legacy smart weapons segment includes the TASER devices used by law enforcement as a safer alternative to firearms. Axon's TASERs (like the latest TASER 10) are the industry standard, with few serious competitors thanks to decades of R&D, patents, and entrenched customer training programs. After TASERs, the company expanded into sensors and cameras: body-worn cameras (Axon Body series), in-car dash cameras (Fleet), and other sensors that capture critical video and audio evidence. These devices are deeply integrated with Axon's software platform, Axon Evidence. Axon's body cams are considered best-in-class and benefit from network effects. Agencies prefer the vendor that partners with their TASERs and back-end software.

To process the vast amounts of data Axon devices generate, the company developed a suite of cloud-based software, including Evidence.com (digital evidence management), Axon Records (reporting and records management), Axon Respond (real-time operations), and AI tools like auto-transcription (Draft). These software offerings allow police and other customers to securely store, manage, and analyse video and data from Axon's devices. Axon sells many products as bundled subscriptions (e.g. Officer Safety Plans) that combine hardware refreshes with ongoing software access, creating sticky recurring revenue. Axon Draft, its AI-powered transcription tool, automates report writing from officer footage and audio. It reduces manual paperwork and feeds directly into Axon Records, strengthening the software ecosystem.

Axon has also invested in Dedrone (counter-drone systems) and launched products like Axon Air (drones) and VR training simulators. These moves extend Axon's reach into emergency dispatch centres, enterprise security, and federal applications, significantly enlarging its addressable market.

The company keeps expanding into adjacent domains. Recently, Axon acquired Prepared Live and agreed to acquire Carbyne to build Axon 911, a modern cloud-based 911 dispatch and communications platform. This marks the company's latest initiative in emergency dispatch. Axon's vision is to replace outdated computer-aided dispatch (CAD) systems with a real-time, cloud-native platform that connects callers, dispatchers, and first responders through live video, text, GPS, and multimedia sharing.

CEO Rick Smith gave the example that Today, if you call 911 in a retailer like a Walmart, the people in the store have no idea you've called 911. They're completely unaware that there might be some incident happening. When we tie together Prepared, Carbyne, Fusus, and our enterprise customers, we can begin to have them collaborate in real time so that they're getting alerts as well and being aware, hey, if there's a 911 call in our parking lot. We're going to need to engage with that or in the store as well. So again, this is where each of these pieces all sort of build on each other into one highly integrated nervous system.

The strategic edge is in its integration. Axon 911 feeds directly into Axon Respond, Evidence.com, and the company's sensor suite, allowing agencies to track calls from the initial 911 contact through officer dispatch, field response, and evidence capture.

This gives Axon control of the full lifecycle. From the moment a call is made to the final courtroom report. If Axon executes, 911 becomes not just another product, but the front door to its entire ecosystem, pulling in new customers and locking in existing ones more deeply. It also expands Axon's TAM significantly, opening the door to municipal contracts and recurring SaaS revenue well beyond traditional law enforcement.

Total Addressable Market

Axon now estimates its total addressable market (TAM) to be around $159 billion, after factoring in international expansion, enterprise customers, and new product categories such as AI and drones. This is orders of magnitude larger than its current revenue of $2.6 billion over the trailing twelve months. By the company's analysis, it has penetrated not even 2% of its global TAM so far, and even in its core U.S. state and local law enforcement segment, Axon remains under 15% penetration.

The company has started selling to federal agencies, corrections, fire and EMS, and even large enterprises. Notably, last year Axon signed its largest-ever deal with a private sector customer (a global logistics company) for body cameras. This underscores how Axon's markets are expanding. Any organisation with public-facing workers or security needs could become a customer. International growth is another vector, and management sees it as the biggest TAM opportunity. Currently, international revenue accounts for only 16% of Axon's total, leaving a significant growth runway. Axon is making inroads with police forces in Europe, Asia, and other regions. Eight in ten police officers in the United Kingdom stated that body-worn cameras make officers feel safer, make their jobs easier, and improve public trust.

Over time, management envisions Axon's platform as ubiquitous in public safety worldwide. Given the combination of a broad product portfolio, high customer lock-in, and a massively under-penetrated market, Axon appears to have a long growth runway ahead.

Axon Draft, its AI-powered transcription tool, automates report writing from officer footage and audio. It reduces manual paperwork and feeds directly into Axon Records, strengthening the software ecosystem. The result is strong customer retention.

Beyond switching costs, Axon enjoys significant intangible assets. Axon's ongoing involvement in setting training standards and engaging with regulators also expands its moat. In my opinion, it would be very hard for a new entrant to displace Axon's weapons or cloud platform, given the lengthy sales cycles and relationships required with government agencies. Competitors do exist in niches such as Motorola Solutions offers competing body cams and dispatch software, and smaller firms offer point solutions like gunshot detection, but no other company provides the full ecosystem of connected devices and software that Axon does.

Financials

Axon's third quarter 2025 results represent its rapid growth, but also reveal pressure on margins and earnings. Revenue for the quarter was $711 million, up 31% year over year (YoY). This marks Axon's seventh consecutive quarter of over 30% growth, a remarkable streak, especially for a company now running at a $2.7 billion annual rate. Product segments still account for the heavy bulk of the business, but services are growing faster. The core TASER product line generated $238 million (up 17% YoY), and sensors delivered $107 million (up 20%). The fastest growth came from Axon's new hardware categories called Platform Solutions, including Fleet car cams, drones, and more, which grew 71% to $61 million. Meanwhile, the Software & Services segment (Axon's cloud subscriptions and digital evidence management) saw revenue of $305 million, soaring 41% YoY. This reflects strong uptake of Axon's premium software features and user growth. Annual recurring revenue (ARR) hit $1.3 billion, also up 41%, with net revenue retention of 124%, indicating that existing customers are expanding their spend.

Source: Author

Despite the robust revenue, Axon was unprofitable on a GAAP basis. The company recorded a net loss of $2 million (about -$0.03 per share). This broke a 14-quarter streak of GAAP profitability. The net loss, in part, explains the stock's sharp drop, in my opinion. The problem was margin compression. Axon's gross margin for hardware (Connected Devices) fell to about 49% this quarter, driven mainly by higher tariffs. This was the first full quarter in which U.S. import tariffs impacted Axon's cost of goods, creating a noticeable drag on profitability. Management noted the hit would impact margins only once, essentially calling it a one-time reset that is now baked in going forward. Services gross margin reached 75% this quarter. With software growing faster than hardware, and as it gradually takes a larger share of the revenue mix, overall gross margins should begin to expand from here. This creates a long-term tailwind for margins, after the gross margin drop this quarter. It's worth noting that Axon's GAAP results include very large stock-based compensation (SBC) expenses, which pushed GAAP EPS into the red. Even so, the profit shortfall came from a jump in operating expenses, driven by higher research and development (R&D) spending (to develop new products like Axon 911, Fleet 3, etc.) and the company's broad-based employee stock vesting program. Adjusted EBITDA came in at $177 million, a 25% margin.

Axon's key metrics remain robust. As mentioned, ARR is $1.3 billion, up 41% YoY, showing the strength of the subscription side of the business. Remaining performance obligations (backlog) grew to $11.4 billion, up 39% YoY, and growing faster than revenue. This equates to roughly 4.5 Axon's current annual revenue. This backlog reflects multi-year contracts, with Axon expecting to recognise 2025% of that $11.4 billion within the next 12 months and the rest over the following decade. Based solely on backlog, revenue would increase roughly 11% in the next 12 months compared to the trailing twelve months, not including new business.

Axon's financial position is solid. As of the third quarter, the company held $2.4 billion in cash, equivalents, and short-term investments. Debt consists of convertible notes and a new senior note, totalling about $2.0 billion in principal. Net of debt, Axon still holds around $436 million in net cash on the balance sheet.

Axon generated $60 million in operating cash flow in Q3, although for the first nine months of the year, it generated negative $6 million. The decline in operating cash was attributed to working capital swings. After capital expenditures, free cash flow (FCF) was $33 million for the quarter. Year to date, FCF is negative, indicating a lot of earnings are being reinvested or tied up in working capital. One item that stands out in Axon's financials is the heavy use of SBC. For the quarter, it was $146 million, or 21% of revenue. Axon forecasts full-year 2025 SBC expense of $580$630 million. Yet, Axon's diluted share count is now about 78 million, flat from a year ago and down quarter over quarter.

Valuation

Axon's stock has always traded at a rich multiple but deservedly so, in my opinion. Even after the post-earnings pullback, the stock trades around $521.99 per share, giving it a market cap of $41.2 billion.

Due to the unprofitable quarter and management prioritising R&D over margins or earnings, the traditional metric (price-to-earnings) isn't useful here. The more appropriate metrics are based on sales or enterprise value (EV).

Source: Gurufocus

Axon's forward price-to-sales (P/S) ratio is 12.6x. Before the dip, it was about 21x. That's still a stretched sales multiple in any context, but the nature of the business provides some assurance. Axon's enterprise value is roughly $40.9 billion (market cap plus debt minus cash). With an expected adjusted EBITDA of about $685 million for 2025, the stock trades around 60x EV/EBITDA. Every metric we can look at is stretched, but I believe the company provides some margin of safety when you focus on the fundamentals that matter, which deserve these higher multiples.

Source: Gurufocus

The 50x forward EV/EBITDA is well below Axon's historical mean of 76x and approaching the low end of its range. That matters. While the multiple remains expensive in absolute terms, it's cheaper than it has been through most of the last few years. I'd argue the business is stronger today than it was when the multiple was peaking.

Source: Author

Revenue has increased at a compound annual growth rate of 31%. Axon deals primarily with U.S. state and local law enforcement agencies. As mentioned above, these relationships are stable, and Axon's switching costs are brutal. Once a department has trained its workforce on Axon hardware and cloud platforms, there's little incentive to switch. And customers aren't switching. They're spending more. Net revenue retention has been increasing every year and now sits at 124%. ARR and backlog are also growing faster than revenue. That means customers aren't just renewing, they're committing to longer, larger contracts.

It's hard to find true peers for Axon because of its complete ecosystem. Motorola Solutions , for example, sells communications and video equipment to public safety agencies and trades at around 5x forward sales and 17x forward EV/EBITDA. Management noted on the call that despite rising competition in certain segments, they see no material headwinds. Customers want the best products, and Axon still makes the best products.

Tyler Technologies , a government software provider, trades around 9x forward sales and roughly 28x forward EV/EBITDA. While both Motorola and Tyler sell into public sector budgets, neither offers the integrated platform that Axon does. Their ecosystems are less cohesive, their switching costs are lower, and they're growing at single digits.

One company that, while not a competitor, is sometimes compared to Axon is Palantir . Both operate in public safety and national security, and both land large U.S. government contracts. Palantir is growing faster and has become an AI favourite, but it trades at 65x forward sales and 124x forward EV/EBITDA, multiples Axon has never reached.

I believe the market has rewarded Axon's accelerating growth and rising software mix with a higher multiple. The current valuation assumes Axon can sustain high growth and expand margins over time. On the Rule of 40, Axon scores a 56 (31% growth plus 25% adjusted EBITDA margin), well above the 40-point threshold most SaaS investors use.

The stock's recent decline may actually be a healthy reset. After the drop, Axon is slightly less overvalued, but its fundamentals continue to improve.

In the last quarter, more gurus reduced or closed positions than added. While their stakes were small, Baillie Gifford (Trades, Portfolio), Jefferies Group (Trades, Portfolio), Frank Sands (Trades, Portfolio), among others, all trimmed exposure. Renaissance Technologies (Trades, Portfolio), Joel Greenblatt (Trades, Portfolio), and Paul Tudor Jones (Trades, Portfolio) were among the few who added.

Risks and Challenges

While Axon's ecosystem is robust, the company faces several risks. I've praised the reliability of government revenue, but that same dependence brings exposure to the often-cyclical budgets of state and local agencies. While the subscription model and long contract terms help smooth out some of that volatility, tighter fiscal environments could delay the adoption of premium software features or lead to slower renewals.

Axon's margin profile has come under pressure, and investors should expect it to remain volatile. The company missed GAAP earnings estimates as the business prioritised R&D and expansion. Axon is actively expanding its TAM by entering new verticals. It is also integrating Prepared Live and Carbyne to build out Axon 911. This comes at a cost. Expenses are increasing, and management made it clear on the call that they do not intend to slow down spending. That's the price of growth. I believe they're playing the long game and making the right call, but it pushes GAAP earnings out and creates short-term pressure on margins.

Tariffs are another concern. The hardware margin drop this quarter was largely driven by U.S.-China tariffs. If those remain in place (or get worse) Axon's hardware profitability could stay structurally lower. The company can try to mitigate the impact through supply chain adjustments or pass some of the cost to customers, but it may not fully offset the hit. Inflation in components or manufacturing could further pressure margins.

Conclusion

I continue to view Axon as a high-quality growth company with an enviable market position and a mission that genuinely makes the world safer. The fundamentals (revenue growth, ARR, and customer retention) are outstanding, and the long-term opportunity remains significant as Axon continues to penetrate its $150 plus billion TAM. That said, I remain mindful of the risks. The valuation leaves little room for error. Still, I believe a company with Axon's ecosystem strength and competitive edge warrants a higher multiple than its peers.

Among the companies I follow, Axon stands out as having one of the strongest moats and most durable competitive positions. It's the kind of business I'm always willing to add on pullbacks. I am a shareholder and have increased my position. I also believe that even a small position can be a useful way to stay close to the story and monitor execution over time.

data.title: FTSE Straits Times Index Closes 0.2% Higher at 4509.34
Intesa CEO warns Rome not to tax banks excessively, stresses public debt role
data.description:
Intesa Sanpaolo CEO Carlo Messina urged Italy's government not to single out banks with excessive tax rises on Thursday, stressing the support they provide in buying public debt.

Messina, who leads Italy's largest lender, also told Il Sole 24 Ore in an interview that banks faced regulatory pressure to reduce their holdings of domestic debt.

Italy is considering raising further the IRAP corporate tax levied on banks and insurers to fund costly amendments to next year's budget, political sources told Reuters on Wednesday.

The draft budget already includes a two-percentage-point IRAP increase for lenders.

Messina said banks and insurers were less than half the total in a group of 22 Italian companies with more than 1 billion euros ($1.2 billion) in annual profit, including some state-owned firms.

"Why not think of a wider target when it comes to supporting public finances?" he said.

Rome is evaluating further hiking IRAP by half a percentage point on bigger lenders only, the sources said.

Messina said that weakening banks was a risky strategy given the support they provided to Italy's economy and urged the government to tackle "shameful" levels of tax evasion instead.

Banks hold around 400 billion euros of Italy's 3 trillion euros in public debt and insurers another 250 billion, Messina said.

"I find the attitude towards the banking system wrong for a country that still needs to solve the problem of its public debt," he said.

"Banks and insurers had in the past, still do and will continue to have a key role in supporting public finances. It would be good not to forget that," he added.

($1 = 0.8630 euros)

data.title: CoreWeave Just Crashed 45% and Cathie Wood Is Buying -- Should You?
data.description:
Ark Invest boosted its exposure to CoreWeave on Monday, stepping in after the AI-infrastructure provider's shares retreated sharply over the past month.

The bulk of the buying occurred in the ARK Innovation ETF (ARKK), which added 396,178 shares. Following the move, ARKK's stake is valued at roughly $52.4 million, representing about 0.7% of the fund.

The ARK Next Generation Internet ETF (ARKW) increased its position as well, acquiring 41,167 shares. ARKW now holds approximately $35.8 million in CoreWeave, making the stock its 19th-largest weight at about 1.7% of assets.The investment firm purchased a combined 437,345 CoreWeave shares across two of its active ETFs.

CoreWeave has lost close to 45% over the last month amid cooling sentiment toward high-growth AI names. Ark has historically used sharp drawdowns to accumulate positions it views as tied to long-term technology themes, including advanced compute infrastructure and cloud-based acceleration services.

Is CRWV Stock a Buy?

Based on the one year price targets offered by 26 analysts, the average target price for CoreWeave Inc is $131.80 with a high estimate of $234.00 and a low estimate of $36.00. The average target implies a upside of +84.87% from the current price of $71.29.

Based on the consensus recommendation from 31 brokerage firms, CoreWeave Inc's average brokerage recommendation is currently 2.5, indicating a "Outperform" status. The rating scale ranges from 1 to 5, where 1 signifies Strong Buy, and 5 denotes Sell.

data.title: Italian Consumer Morale Weakens to 7-Month Low
data.description:
The Italian consumer confidence index fell to 95 in November 2025, from an eight-month high of 97.6 in the previous month.

This marked the lowest reading since April, signaling a broad deterioration in sentiment among households.

The personal climate index dropped to 94.5 from 97 in October, the consumer economic climate fell to 96.5 from 99.3, the current climate index slipped to 98.6 from 100.2, while the future climate index decreased to 90.2 from 94.1.

Meanwhile, assessments of saving opportunities and unemployment expectations also continued to deteriorate.

data.title: SNG: Listed firms in Bahrain post strong Q3 gains
data.description:
Staff Writer

Listed companies in Bahrain delivered a strong performance in the third quarter of 2025, with aggregate net profits soaring 31.3 per cent year-on-year to reach $597 million.

Analysis by Kuwait-base Kamco Invest shows that the robust quarterly result was broad-based, as 12 out of 14 sectors, including major contributors like banking and materials, reported year-on-year growth.

For the first nine months of the year (9M-2025), total net earnings for Bahrain Bourse also showed a solid increase, rising 9.7pc year-on-year to $1.6 billion (from $1.4bn in 9M-2024).

The vital banking sector was a key driver, seeing a 16.4pc year-on-year rise in Q3-2025 profits to $222.4m.

All eight listed banks contributed to this growth. For the nine-month period, the sector’s aggregate net earnings improved 14.7pc to $739.6m.

Al Salam Bank was instrumental in the 9M-2025 growth, reporting a 37.4pc jump in net profits to $154.3m, driven by its core banking operations.

Conversely, while reporting the largest net profits among banks at $203.9m for 9M-2025, Bank ABC saw a marginal 5.1pc drop in its nine-month earnings, despite its total operating income rising to $1.033bn.

In the materials sector, sole listed company Alba reported a strong Q3-2025, with net profits increasing 23.3pc year-on-year to $178.4m.

This was primarily attributed to an 11pc rise in LME prices during the quarter.

However, Alba’s 9M-2025 net profits declined 25.5pc to $291.6m, as higher feedstock costs during the year offset the average selling price increase.

The diversified financial sector saw profits nearly triple in Q3-2025, reaching $108.8m.

The nine-month performance was similarly impressive, with net earnings surging 71.1pc to $186.4m.

GFH Financial Group was the main support, reporting higher 9M profits of $168.5m.

Against the backdrop of widespread growth, the telecom sector recorded a slight 4.5pc year-on-year drop in Q3-2025 to $48.7m.

This decline was mainly driven by Beyon, which reported a profit decrease to $44.3m, attributed to additional taxes from the domestic minimum top-up tax (DMTT) effective this year.

In contrast, Zain Bahrain saw a marginal 1pc increase in its Q3 net earnings to $4.34m.

Zooming out, aggregate net profits for companies listed on GCC exchanges rose 7.9pc year-on-year in the third quarter of 2025 to a recent high of $65.6bn, up from $60.7bn.

The surge was primarily driven by strong profit growth in the Banking and Real Estate sectors, offsetting declines in telecom and utilities.

Banking profits set a new record high at $17.4bn, with double-digit growth in the UAE (+25.1pc) and Saudi Arabia (+15.2pc) outweighing a decline in Qatar.

Real Estate net profits surged by nearly two-thirds, led by the UAE, where profits grew 43.1pc to $2.67bn.

Meanwhile, the energy sector saw a marginal 0.5pc profit increase to $28.9bn, despite a 13.7pc drop in average Brent crude prices.

All GCC markets reported positive year-on-year profit growth in Q3.

Saudi-listed companies posted their highest profits in five quarters at $38.2bn.

For the first nine months of the year, Saudi-listed companies saw profits drop 5.3pc to $107.5bn, mainly due to declines in energy, materials, and insurance.

Copyright 2022 Al Hilal Publishing and Marketing Group Provided by SyndiGate Media Inc. (Syndigate.info).

data.title: REG - Atlantic Lithium Ltd - Result of Annual General Meeting
data.description:
RNS Number : 2732J Atlantic Lithium Limited 27 November 2025  

27 November 2025

Result of AGM

Atlantic Lithium Limited (AIM: ALL, ASX: A11, GSE: ALLGH, "Atlantic Lithium" or the "Company"), the Africa-focused lithium exploration and development company targeting the delivery of Ghana's first lithium mine, advises that all Resolutions put to shareholders at the Company's Annual General Meeting ("AGM"), held earlier today, were passed by way of a poll.

As previously announced, Resolution 6 was withdrawn prior to the meeting being held following a review of proxy instructions that had been received, which indicated that the resolution would not receive sufficient support to pass.

In accordance with Listing Rule 3.13.2 and section 251AA(2) of the Corporations Act 2001 (Cth), details of the results and the proxies received in respect of each resolution can be found via the following link: http://www.rns-pdf.londonstockexchange.com/rns/2732J_1-2025-11-27.pdf.

Authorised for release by Amanda Harsas, Finance Director and Company Secretary, Atlantic Lithium Limited.

For any further information, please contact:

About Atlantic Lithium

www.atlanticlithium.com.au

Atlantic Lithium is an AIM, ASX, and GSE-listed lithium company advancing its flagship project, the Ewoyaa Lithium Project, a lithium spodumene pegmatite discovery in Ghana, through to production to become the country's first lithium-producing mine.

The Company published a Definitive Feasibility Study in respect of the Project in July 2023. The Project was awarded a Mining Lease in October 2023, an Environmental Protection Authority ("EPA") Permit in September 2024, and a Mine Operating Permit in October 2024 and is being developed under an earn-in agreement with Elevra Lithium Inc.

Atlantic Lithium holds a portfolio of lithium projects within 509km2 and 771km2 of granted and under-application tenure across Ghana and Côte d'Ivoire respectively, which, in addition to the Project, comprises significantly under-explored, highly prospective licences.

data.title: REG - VietNam Holding Ltd - Estimated Daily Net Asset Value
data.description:
RNS Number : 2711J VietNam Holding Limited 27 November 2025  

For immediate release

VietNam Holding Limited ("VNH" or the "Company")

Estimated Daily Net Asset Value (NAV)

The Company announces that, as of the close of business on 27 Nov 2025, its Estimated NAV was USD 105.7 million or USD 5.486 per share and GBP 79.9 million or GBP 4.148 per share (GBP/USD = 1.3226).

Change in position as of 27 Nov 2025:

NAV per share (USD)

Vietnam All Share Index (USD)

Daily

-0.2%

0.0%

Calendar month-to-date

-2.5%

1.2%

Calendar year-to-date

4.8%

30.9%

1-year

8.7%

34.8%

3-year CAGR

19.4%

23.0%

5-year CAGR

13.1%

11.3%

10-year CAGR

9.5%

10.4%

15-year CAGR

9.7%

7.9%

Since inception CAGR

5.6%

N/A

Daily performance is calculated by reference to the previous day NAV/share and VNAS (Vietnam All Share Index). Calendar month-to-date performance is calculated by reference to NAV/share and VNAS as of 31 Oct 2025. Calendar year-to-date performance is calculated by reference to NAV/share and VNAS at the previous calendar year end (31 Dec 2024). 1-year performance is calculated by reference to NAV/share and VNAS as of 27 Nov 2024. 3-year CAGR (compound annual growth rate) is calculated by reference to NAV/share and VNAS as of 27 Nov 2022. 5-year CAGR is calculated by reference to NAV/share and VNAS as of 27 Nov 2020. 10-year CAGR is calculated by reference to NAV/share and VNAS as of 27 Nov 2015. 15-year CAGR is calculated by reference to NAV/share and VNAS as of 27 Nov 2010. Since inception CAGR is calculated by reference to NAV/share as of 30 Jun 2006. VNAS is sourced from Bloomberg, which uses back-tested positions for VNAS for before 27 Jan 2014 as released by HoSE (Ho Chi Minh City Stock Exchange).

The Estimated Daily NAV stated in this announcement is based on estimated valuations and accordingly the Company accepts no responsibility for the accuracy of the Estimated Daily NAV figure given, and neither is any responsibility implied. The actual month end NAV in any month may vary from the Estimated Daily NAV provided in the relevant month. Accordingly, no reliance should be placed on the Estimated Daily NAV, and it has been provided for indicative purposes only.

More information on the Company is available at www.vietnamholding.com

Craig Martin

craig@dynamcapital.com

Tel.: +84 28 38277 590

Dynam Capital Limited

https://www.dynamcapital.com
